_doc: The P7 / clock-invariance instrument (slice MC) — the sibling of the 32-run conservation sweep. Run: dotnet run --project src/Inspector -c Release -- sweep docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-17-clock-invariance-experiment.json . Read runs/sweeps/clock-invariance/clock-divergence.csv: one row per variant x seed x metric, with frame=nominal|real kept separate. P7 says YearsPerEpoch is an integration step and nothing more, so every ratio_25y_to_1y here should be 1.0. worldYears is the span; the runner DERIVES each clock's epoch count from it, so the three clocks always cover the same 250 world-years. Never add 'epochs' to this file - it is refused, and it is the trap that made every throwaway P7 harness disagree.
name: clock-invariance
_seeds_doc: Seeds 13 and 777 are dead-world controls (1 port at every clock): they must report ~1.0x, and if they ever diverge the instrument is measuring itself. 42, 99 and 11 are the large worlds where the two prior harnesses disagreed.
_variants_doc: baseline is the stock config (the product). mints-off removes the monetary feedback loop and is the frame every prior P7 table was measured in - it is what the 2026-07-17 baseline and seed-99 specs must be reconciled against.
